title: Xtra Chewy Algerian Narutomaki Tagine
description: A sweet and chewy one-pot Algerian dish featuring narutomaki, yakimono-grill, and paleo-friendly ingredients, this tagine is a unique blend of flavors and textures. It is perfect for a special occasion or a cozy night in.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the sliced narutomaki and cook for 2 minutes on each side, or until it is lightly browned.
Add the cumin, coriander, and cinnamon and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the honey and chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the narutomaki is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the tagine from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Serve the narutomaki and sauce over couscous or cauliflower rice, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
